---
title: "Raona Lead Scoring"
format:
  html:
    toc: false
---

<!-- Top accent bar -->
<div class="top-bar"></div>

<!-- ===== ACT 1: HERO ===== -->
<div class="section-hero">

<div class="section-label">Nuclio Digital School -- Trabajo Final de Master</div>

# Thousands of messages. Less than 6% get a reply.

<div class="subtitle">
Raona is a Microsoft Gold Partner in Barcelona that contacts thousands of potential B2B clients
through LinkedIn and email. This project uses data science to identify <strong>who</strong> to
contact, with <strong>which service</strong>, through <strong>which channel</strong>,
and <strong>when</strong>.
</div>

<div class="meta">
Adriana Caballero &middot; Data Science &middot; March 2026
</div>

</div>

---

<!-- ===== ACT 2: THE SCALE OF THE PROBLEM ===== -->

<div class="section-label">The data</div>

## 10,946 outreach conversations. One question: who will reply?

Raona's sales team uses an automated outreach tool to contact prospects via LinkedIn and email.
Each contact receives a sequence of messages. The dataset captures every conversation from
their Spain-focused campaigns.

<div class="stat-row">
<div class="stat-card">
  <div class="label">Total contacts</div>
  <div class="value">10,946</div>
</div>
<div class="stat-card">
  <div class="label">Replied</div>
  <div class="value gold">632</div>
  <div class="detail">5.8% reply rate</div>
</div>
<div class="stat-card">
  <div class="label">Campaigns</div>
  <div class="value">45+</div>
  <div class="detail">Across 7 product lines</div>
</div>
<div class="stat-card">
  <div class="label">Features engineered</div>
  <div class="value">33</div>
  <div class="detail">Raw + AI + NLP + External</div>
</div>
</div>

### The funnel

<!-- TODO: Insert funnel chart from NB02 -->
<!-- {{< embed ../notebooks/02_eda.ipynb#fig-funnel >}} -->

The conversion funnel reveals where prospects drop off. LinkedIn significantly outperforms
email as a response channel for B2B outreach in this context.

---

<!-- ===== ACT 3: DISCOVERY ===== -->

<div class="section-label">Exploratory analysis</div>

## What distinguishes a contact who replies?

Before building any model, we explored the data to understand what makes a converter different
from the 94% who never respond.

### Key findings

<div class="insight-card">
  <div class="insight-title">Seniority matters, but not linearly</div>
  Directors and managers reply more often than C-level executives or junior profiles.
  The sweet spot is mid-to-senior decision makers.
</div>

<div class="insight-card">
  <div class="insight-title">Company size has a U-shape</div>
  Both mid-size (50-250 employees) and large enterprises (1000+) show higher reply rates
  than small companies. Very large organizations have dedicated teams that evaluate proposals.
</div>

<div class="insight-card">
  <div class="insight-title">LinkedIn dominates email</div>
  LinkedIn reply rate is significantly higher than email. The personal, professional context
  of LinkedIn messages resonates better with B2B prospects.
</div>

<div class="insight-card">
  <div class="insight-title">Thursday is the best day</div>
  Reply rates peak on Thursdays. Monday and Friday show the lowest engagement,
  suggesting mid-week outreach is optimal.
</div>

### Converter vs non-converter profiles

<!-- TODO: Insert industry/seniority comparison charts from NB02 -->

### FIT scores by product line

<!-- TODO: Insert FIT violin plots from NB02 -->

---

<!-- ===== ACT 4: METHODOLOGY ===== -->

<div class="section-label">Methodology</div>

## From raw data to actionable scores

The project follows CRISP-DM methodology across five notebooks, transforming raw outreach
data into a production-ready scoring system.

<div class="pipeline">
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">01</div>
    <div class="step-label">Data Loading<br>& Cleaning</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">02</div>
    <div class="step-label">Exploratory<br>Analysis</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">03</div>
    <div class="step-label">Feature Engineering<br>& NLP</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">04</div>
    <div class="step-label">ML<br>Models</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">05</div>
    <div class="step-label">MLOps<br>& Deploy</div>
  </div>
</div>

### Feature engineering

33 features from four sources, each with a clear prefix indicating its origin:

| Origin | Prefix | Count | Examples |
|--------|--------|-------|---------|
| Raw from Raona | *(none)* | ~10 | Years in role, Number of employees, Industry |
| AI-enriched by Enginy | `ai_` | ~8 | ai_SENIORITY, ai_FIT, ai_TYPE_OF_CONTACT |
| Engineered by us | `fe_` | ~8 | fe_company_size_bucket, fe_headcount_momentum |
| External enrichment | `ext_` | ~3 | ext_ms_maturity_score, ext_has_competitor_tech |
| NLP from text reports | `nlp_` | ~6 | nlp_embedding_01..03, nlp_topic_cluster |

### NLP pipeline

Text fields (CONTACT REPORT, COMPANY REPORT, MOMENTUM) were processed with:

- **Sentence-BERT** (multilingual MiniLM) for embeddings, reduced to 3 dimensions via UMAP
- **BERTopic** for topic modeling, identifying thematic clusters
- **Keyword extraction** for urgency signals and decision-maker indicators

---

<!-- ===== ACT 5: MODELS & RESULTS ===== -->

<div class="section-label">Models</div>

## Four models, one system

### Model 1: Lead Scoring

The core model predicts which contacts are most likely to reply. LightGBM was selected
after comparing Logistic Regression, XGBoost, and LightGBM, tuned with Optuna (50 trials).

<div class="stat-row">
<div class="stat-card">
  <div class="label">PR-AUC</div>
  <div class="value gold">0.281</div>
  <div class="detail">Primary metric (5x random baseline)</div>
</div>
<div class="stat-card">
  <div class="label">ROC-AUC</div>
  <div class="value">0.807</div>
  <div class="detail">Strong discrimination</div>
</div>
<div class="stat-card">
  <div class="label">Precision@100</div>
  <div class="value">19%</div>
  <div class="detail">3.3x lift over random</div>
</div>
</div>

<!-- TODO: Insert cumulative gains / lift curve from NB04 -->

<div class="highlight-box">
<p><strong>Business impact:</strong> If Raona contacts only the top 10% of scored leads,
they capture approximately 35% of all positive replies -- a <strong>3.6x improvement</strong>
over random selection.</p>
</div>

### Feature importance (SHAP)

<!-- TODO: Insert SHAP summary plot from NB04 -->

The top predictive features by origin:

| Origin | SHAP contribution | Interpretation |
|--------|------------------|----------------|
| NLP features | ~52% | Text report length and embeddings dominate |
| Raw features | ~29% | Connections, years in role, company size |
| Engineered features | ~19% | Seniority encoding, headcount momentum |
| External features | <1% | Microsoft maturity score |

### Robustness analysis: detecting data leakage

<div class="highlight-box">
<p><strong>Data leakage detected:</strong> Raona enriches contacts <em>after</em> they reply,
not before. This means <code>nlp_contact_report_length</code> (80% coverage for positives
vs 39% for negatives), <code>fe_has_email</code>, and <code>Years in role</code> are
<strong>consequences of the target</strong>, not genuine predictors.</p>
</div>

A **robust model** trained without the 3 leaking features shows PR-AUC of 0.159 (-43%).
This confirms which features have genuine predictive power independent of post-response enrichment.

**Two-model strategy:**

- **Complete model** (all features): for retrospective analysis of already-enriched contacts.
  Demonstrates the NLP pipeline and has academic value.
- **Robust model** (without leaking features): the correct production model for scoring
  new contacts that haven't been enriched yet. Used in the API, Airflow pipeline, and Batch Scorer.

Detecting and handling this data leakage demonstrates critical thinking about the data generation
process -- a key skill in applied data science.

### Model 2: Segmentation

K-Means clustering (k=7, silhouette=0.138) identifies distinct prospect segments:

<!-- TODO: Insert segment profiles chart -->

| Segment | Profile | Reply rate | Key insight |
|---------|---------|-----------|-------------|
| 0 | Financial Services, senior | 9% | Strong fit for DATA & INFRA |
| 1 | Telecom & Tech, director level | 8% | Largest segment (2,400 contacts) |
| 2 | Large Telecom, high engagement | 11% | Above-average conversion |
| 3 | Enterprise / Government, 6K+ employees | 9% | Largest companies |
| 4 | Junior profiles, Gov & Education | 7% | Limited decision-making power |
| 5 | Industrial SMEs, low response | 3% | Lowest conversion rate |
| 6 | Pharma & Wellness, top converters | **12%** | Best performing segment |

### Model 3: Product recommendation

Validation of existing FIT scores against actual campaign engagement. The model recommends
which Raona product line to pitch based on the contact and company profile.

### Model 4: Channel & timing

- **LinkedIn** consistently outperforms email across all segments
- **Thursday** shows the highest reply rate; Monday and Friday the lowest
- Segment-level channel preferences inform outreach strategy

---

<!-- ===== ACT 6: MLOps ===== -->

<div class="section-label">Production</div>

## We built a system, not just a model

The scoring pipeline is deployed as a production-ready system with API, containerization,
experiment tracking, and drift monitoring.

<div class="pipeline">
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">API</div>
    <div class="step-label">FastAPI<br>endpoint</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">Airflow</div>
    <div class="step-label">Orchestrated<br>pipeline</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">Docker</div>
    <div class="step-label">Containerized<br>deployment</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">MLflow</div>
    <div class="step-label">Experiment<br>tracking</div>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">PSI</div>
    <div class="step-label">Drift<br>monitoring</div>
  </div>
</div>

- **FastAPI** serves predictions via `POST /score` -- returns lead score, segment, product
  recommendation, and channel suggestion
- **Apache Airflow** orchestrates the full ML pipeline: ingest, transform, score, monitor,
  and conditional retraining ([pipeline details](mlops_pipeline.html))
- **Docker** packages the full stack (API + Airflow) for reproducible deployment
- **MLflow** tracks all experiments with metrics, parameters, and model artifacts
- **PSI monitoring** detects feature drift to trigger model retraining

### Streamlit dashboard

A three-tab interactive application for the sales team:

1. **Dashboard** -- KPIs, score distribution, cumulative gains, channel analysis,
   timing insights, and segment profiles
2. **Lead Scorer** -- Input a contact's profile and get real-time score, segment
   assignment, and outreach recommendation. Uses robust model by default;
   toggle to complete model if the contact is already enriched.
3. **Batch Scorer** -- Upload a CSV of contacts, score them all with the robust model,
   and download ranked results with priority assignments.

<!-- TODO: Insert Streamlit screenshots -->

---

<!-- ===== ACT 7: IMPACT ===== -->

<div class="section-label">Impact</div>

## What changes for Raona

<div class="stat-row">
<div class="stat-card">
  <div class="label">Before</div>
  <div class="value">5.8%</div>
  <div class="detail">Reply rate (random outreach)</div>
</div>
<div class="stat-card">
  <div class="label">After (top 10%)</div>
  <div class="value gold">~20%</div>
  <div class="detail">Estimated reply rate with model</div>
</div>
<div class="stat-card">
  <div class="label">Efficiency gain</div>
  <div class="value gold">3.6x</div>
  <div class="detail">Lift over random selection</div>
</div>
</div>

Instead of contacting everyone equally, Raona can now:

1. **Prioritize** -- Score all prospects and focus on the top deciles
2. **Personalize** -- Match product recommendations to each contact's profile
3. **Optimize timing** -- Send outreach on the best-performing days
4. **Segment** -- Tailor messaging per cluster (enterprise vs SME, pharma vs industrial)

---

<!-- ===== LIMITATIONS & FUTURE WORK ===== -->

<div class="section-label">Looking ahead</div>

## Limitations and future work

**Limitations:**

- Selection bias in NLP features (enrichment correlates with response)
- Small positive class (632 out of 10,946) limits model complexity
- Spain-only scope; LATAM market excluded due to insufficient data

**Future work:**

- A/B testing framework to measure real-world impact of model-guided outreach
- Feedback loop for continuous retraining as new conversion data arrives
- Integration with the outreach tool's API for real-time scoring
- Expansion to LATAM market when sufficient data is collected

---

<div class="footer">
Adriana Caballero &middot; Nuclio Digital School &middot; Data Science Master &middot; March 2026
<br>
Supervised by [tutor name]. Built with Python, LightGBM, Streamlit, FastAPI, and Docker.
</div>
